posts.name: Phil T r o t t i e r
posts.cooked:
2018 Henri Germain et Fils Meursault - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Meursault (1/24/2021)
Served too cold initially so the nose was quite muted. After quite a bit of air and time in the glass this became much more outspoken and very enticing. A very nice wine. The nose offers citrus, a bit of stone fruits, some élevage and strong mineral notes (crushed stones). The mouth shows nice balance driven by the tension (citrus) and then a bit of élevage (vanilla cream), minerality and enticing slight bitterness at the end. Although it is very enjoyable right now and quite balanced, I feel that it could develop a little more complexity with time. I should have bought much more of this as I wouldn’t mind drinking it on a regular basis even this young. Very good and this is as good a Meursault Village as I’ve had in some time.
posts.display_username: Phil T r o t t i e r

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Good to see some love for this producer, one of my favorites!

posts.name: Don Cornutt
posts.cooked:
I have the Meursault Charmes 2018 from this producer
A truly excellent white burgundy. Nice note on the village wine.

posts.name: Daniel Silva
posts.cooked: Agreed, think it is a great value tbh. I have it on the btg list on the restaurant I work at on a regular basis, and, while young, it is a great wine for the price and for a Mersault village.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Agreed, think it is a great value tbh. I have it on the btg list on the restaurant I work at on a regular basis, and, while young, it is a great wine for the price and for a Mersault village.

More than great for the price in my opinion! I’d put Germain in any Meursault top five these days.

posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked: Just got an offer for this and other 2018s and the prices reflect William’s sentiment. These days you get 5 minutes with a good value producer before they are selling you $100 village wines.

posts.name: Daniel Silva
posts.cooked:
Agreed, think it is a great value tbh. I have it on the btg list on the restaurant I work at on a regular basis, and, while young, it is a great wine for the price and for a Mersault village.

More than great for the price in my opinion! I’d put Germain in any Meursault top five these days.

Absolutely! And thankfully their importer in Portugal is very respectful of all their clients (producers, business, and end clients) and believes that they should price their wines based on how much they paid to the producer, not necessarily on what they think the market is willing to spend.

posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked: Hunted down a case of the 2019 in EP at £33.75. That’s more like it! The US offer for the 2018 was close to $100.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Just got an offer for this and other 2018s and the prices reflect William’s sentiment. These days you get 5 minutes with a good value producer before they are selling you $100 village wines.

In this case, it’s more like decades than five minutes! I really have no idea why they are not better known.

My predicament as a critic is that if I like a producer and think they deserve more validation, and publish accordingly, the wines go up in price…

posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked: Well this was my number one young white Burg last year. So I can’t complain. I’m more than satisfied with the UK prices! Bought 5 cases of Village and 1er EP.

posts.name: Phil T r o t t i e r
posts.cooked:
Hunted down a case of the 2019 in EP at £33.75. That’s more like it! The US offer for the 2018 was close to $100.

Great price, great grab! The Canadian price is pretty much in line with the US offer you mention at 90$ (albeit in CAD). I saw that there are a few 1er still available (2017 and 2018, some Chassagne-Montrachet as well) in various locations around me so as soon as time allows, I’ll have them join the Village.
posts.display_username: Phil T r o t t i e r

posts.name: Peter Chiu
posts.cooked:
A village wine at CA$ 114.25 is pricey in Montreal, QC.

My prefer Meursault by Boucher is around CA $65 to $ 70.
